task: Update locale and languages: geo-detect user location and auto-select language (Russian, English, Chinese, Arabic). Fix English not working. Fix URL locale switching appending instead of replacing. All main pages translated. Translations in code not DB.

plannerOutput.taskSummary: Replace kz+in locales with zh+ar. Fix three live bugs: (1) language switcher does nothing on click, (2) middleware prepends another locale prefix when a URL already has one causing double-prefix 404s (e.g. /in/en/admin/ID), (3) English cookie never saved so geo-redirect fires on every request. Add geo-detection for CN/TW/HK→zh, MENA→ar, Russian-speaking countries→ru. Full real translations for zh and ar. RTL layout support for Arabic.

bugs.description: Visiting a URL that already has a locale prefix (e.g. /en/admin/ID) causes middleware to prepend geo-detected locale again, producing /in/en/admin/ID → 404. Screenshot confirmed.
bugs.rootCause: middleware.ts line 31: `hasLocalePrefix` regex only matches /(ru|kz|in) — it does NOT match /en since English is the default and has no prefix in normal routing. But when a URL is shared that contains /en/ as a path segment (admin routes can have /en/ in the token), or when any user shares a URL from an /en/-prefixed route, middleware treats it as an unprefixed URL and prepends the geo locale on top.
bugs.fix: Extend `hasLocalePrefix` to match ALL non-default locale prefixes (ru|zh|ar after the migration). Also add a guard: if the path already contains any known locale as a path segment at position 1, skip geo-redirect entirely. After migration the regex becomes /^\/(ru|zh|ar)(\/|$)/. For the /en/ case: the middleware's saved-locale redirect also double-prefixes — if cookie says 'ru' and the user navigates to any path, it blindly prepends /ru/ even if the path already starts with a prefix. Fix: check hasLocalePrefix BEFORE the saved-locale redirect block.

bugs.description: Clicking a language in the Navbar dropdown does nothing — language does not switch.
bugs.rootCause: `switchLocale` in Navbar.tsx line 117 uses `window.location.pathname` and `window.location.href` assignment. The strip regex `/^\/(ru|kz|in)(\/|$)/` only covers old locales. After adding zh/ar any /zh/ or /ar/ prefix would not be stripped. Additionally the function uses `window.location.href` which causes a full page reload — that is intentional — but if the regex fails to strip, it builds a double-prefixed URL. The deeper issue is that `router.push` from next-intl's `useRouter` should be used instead via the `useRouter` hook from `@/navigation` to correctly handle locale switching without raw URL manipulation.
bugs.fix: Replace `switchLocale` entirely: use next-intl's `router.replace` with the `{locale}` option — `router.replace(pathname, { locale })` — where `pathname` comes from next-intl's `usePathname` (already imported as locale-stripped). Also write the `BINAYAH_LOCALE` cookie client-side on switch so middleware honors the preference and does not immediately redirect back. Regex in `switchLocale` also needs updating to include zh|ar.

bugs.description: When a user is geo-redirected to /ru/ or /zh/ and then switches to English, there is no BINAYAH_LOCALE=en cookie written. The next request hits middleware, reads no cookie (or an old non-en cookie), geo-detects again, and redirects them away from English.
bugs.rootCause: middleware.ts: the `hasLocalePrefix` block sets a cookie for non-English locales. The English default path (fall-through to `intlMiddleware`) never sets BINAYAH_LOCALE=en. So switching to English is not persisted.
bugs.fix: In middleware, when falling through to English (the default), also set BINAYAH_LOCALE=en cookie. OR: treat the absence of the cookie differently — only geo-redirect if cookie is entirely absent (first visit). If cookie is present and equals 'en', never redirect. Client-side: when `switchLocale('en')` is called, write `document.cookie = 'BINAYAH_LOCALE=en; path=/; max-age=31536000'` before navigating.

plannerOutput.rtl: When locale=ar, add dir='rtl' to the <html> element in src/app/[locale]/layout.tsx (e.g. <html lang={locale} dir={locale === 'ar' ? 'rtl' : 'ltr'}>). Use CSS logical properties (margin-inline-start/end, padding-inline-start/end) for any new layout added in this task. Existing LTR layout will flip naturally with dir=rtl for flex/grid. The Navbar switchLocale function should also mirror correctly since it uses flexbox. No new Tailwind RTL variants needed for existing components — the dir attribute handles it at the browser level. Test the navbar, homepage hero, property cards, and footer in RTL.
outOfScope: Property names, descriptions, and dynamic content from the API — these come from the DB, not translation files
Translating admin pages (/admin/*) — internal tool, English-only is fine
communities and developers detail pages — API-sourced content
Currency conversion — already a separate UI concern, not part of this task
SEO hreflang meta tags — nice-to-have, not in task scope
Removing old /kz/ and /in/ URL redirects for SEO — not requested
gotchas: localePrefix: 'as-needed' means English has NO /en/ prefix in URLs. So /en/... is NOT a valid route — it will 404. The double-prefix bug occurs because admin tokens can contain 'en' as a substring in the path, which the middleware misreads. Always treat any path that already starts with a known locale prefix as pre-localized.
ALL user-facing pages live under src/app/[locale]/ — never add routes at the top-level src/app/<route>/ as middleware would rewrite to /<locale>/<route> and 404.
Use @/navigation (next-intl wrappers) for Link and useRouter — NOT next/navigation — to preserve locale prefix in all navigation.

switchLocale in Navbar currently uses window.location.href = target (full reload). Switching to router.replace(pathname, { locale }) from next-intl's useRouter is cleaner and avoids raw URL construction bugs entirely.
The BINAYAH_LOCALE cookie must be set for English too (value='en') otherwise the middleware geo-redirect fires on every English-preference visit.

When updating routing.ts locales array from [en,ru,kz,in] to [en,ru,zh,ar], also update: (1) middleware.ts hasLocalePrefix regex, (2) middleware.ts savedLocale redirect guard, (3) middleware.ts GEO_LOCALE_MAP, (4) Navbar LANGUAGES_LIST array, (5) Navbar switchLocale strip regex, (6) all messages/*.json lang sections, (7) messages/kz.json and messages/in.json — these files can be deleted once routing.ts no longer lists them (request.ts dynamically imports by locale name).
Turbopack .next cache can corrupt when files move — orchestrator nukes .next before QA but DEV should also clear it if seeing 500s after locale file changes.
TypeScript strict mode: routing.locales.includes(locale as any) pattern already used in request.ts — keep this pattern, don't use a type assertion that breaks strict mode.

devReport.summary: Replace kz/in locales with zh/ar; fix double-prefix bug, broken switcher, missing English cookie; add RTL for Arabic; full zh+ar translations

designBrief.direction: no aesthetic direction needed for this scope

selfGrade.action: Rewrite middleware.ts — GEO_LOCALE_MAP, LOCALE_PREFIX_REGEX, always write English cookie

src/components/Navbar.tsx: { code: "zh" } and { code: "ar" } in LANGUAGES_LIST

reviewerReport.notes: Two minor issues fixed: sitemap.ts LOCALES updated to zh/ar, dead Navbar in-branch removed. No security issues.

qaReport.notes: All 8 tests passed. No double-prefix, RTL confirmed, old locales 404, cookie persistence OK, zero console errors.

liveTestReport.notes: All 6 live checks passed. Double-prefix fixed, RTL confirmed, old locales 404, English cookie persistence fixed.

summary: Locale overhaul shipped: kz/in replaced by zh/ar, double-prefix bug fixed, English cookie persistence fixed, RTL for Arabic, geo-detection expanded to 26 countries, real translations.

loopContext.designRouteReason: No new UI components; translation + middleware logic + one HTML attribute. Directly to DEV.

contextGaps.missing: PROJECT_CONTEXT.md still documents locales as [en, ru, kz, in]. Update to [en, ru, zh, ar] with defaultLocale=en and rtlLocales=[ar]. Also document that middleware writes BINAYAH_LOCALE cookie for ALL locales including 'en' (prior behavior only wrote for non-default), and that the prefix regex must be /^\/(ru|zh|ar)(\/|$)/.

Add gotcha: the language switcher in Navbar MUST use next-intl's router.replace(pathname, { locale }) — not window.location.href assignment with manual regex — to avoid double-prefix URLs. Also: the BINAYAH_LOCALE cookie must be written client-side in switchLocale BEFORE router.replace, otherwise the next middleware pass may re-redirect.

Add section: Arabic (ar) is RTL. The <html dir> attribute is set in src/app/[locale]/layout.tsx based on locale === 'ar'. For any new layout, prefer CSS logical properties (margin-inline-start/end) over directional ones (ml/mr).
